$comment: Migration drift gate manifest (Phase 0F). This does NOT use the Supabase ledger,
which cannot be matched to repo filenames (the ledger stores CLI timestamps like
20260724120132; repo files use NNN_ prefixes). Instead we verify the ACTUAL SCHEMA.
- Every migration <= baseline was verified applied in Phase 0D (2026-07) and is
foundational (the live app runs on it daily). We do NOT re-verify those.
- Every migration ABOVE the baseline MUST have an entry here (CI enforces this),
with either a `probe` (a SQL SELECT returning a single boolean, TRUE when the
migration's effect is present in the live DB) or `dataOnly: true` (a data-only
migration with no schema artifact to verify).
When you add migration NNN (> baseline): add an entry keyed by its zero-padded number.
Prefer probing a column/constraint/table/function the migration creates. Keep the SQL

058.describes: invoices.seller_address DEFAULT corrected to the Maharashtra address
058.probe: SELECT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='public' AND table_name='invoices' AND column_name='seller_address' AND column_default LIKE '%Andheri East%')

059.probe: SELECT (EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='public' AND table_name='order_items' AND column_name='source') AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='public' AND table_name='order_items' AND column_name='hsn') AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='public' AND table_name='order_items' AND column_name='gst_rate') AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='public' AND table_name='order_items' AND column_name='uqc') AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_constraint WHERE conname='order_items_source_check'))
